description: §19.3.3 E-ERROR-001 — `fail` used in a function NOT declared failable (`!`). notFailable() has no `!` modifier, so `fail MyError.Bad(...)` fires E-ERROR-001. Asserted AS an error (§34 fatal partition). Incidental W-DEAD-FUNCTION/W-PROGRAM-001 ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §19.3.3: `fail` SHALL be valid only inside a function body declared with `!`. Using `fail` without `!` SHALL be E-ERROR-001.
